DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF STONEBRAE CLUB PARTNERS, LLC'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS. MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES IS GRANTED. CCP 2030.270(B) REQUIRES THAT ANY EXTENSION OF A DEADLINE TO RESPOND TO INTERROGATORIES SPECIFY THE EXTENDED DATE. THIS DID NOT OCCUR. BY OPERATION OF CCP 2030.290 THE OBJECTIONS ARE WAIVED. THE LATER MEET AND CONFER CORRESPONDENCE DID NOT PRESERVE THE RIGHT TO OBJECT. THEREFORE, ALL OBJECTIONS ARE WAIVED. WOLLACK'S REFERENCES TO "ANY AND ALL OTHER DAMAGES" AND "ALL OTHER REMEDIES" ARE IMPROPER BECAUSE THEY DO NOT IDENTIFY WHAT THOSE DAMAGES OR REMEDIES ARE. WHILE SOME TYPES OF DAMAGES MAY NOT BE SUBJECT TO PRECISE CALCULATION BY A PARTY (SUCH AS EMOTIONAL DISTRESS DAMAGES ASSESSED BY THE JURY OR FINANCIAL ESTIMATES ASSESSED BY EXPERT ANALYSIS), THE PARTY SEEKING THOSE DAMAGES MUST IDENTIFY THEM SO THE RESPONDING PARTY CAN PREPARE. THE REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED IN PART. FROM THE CORRESPONDENCE, IT APPEARS THAT THE PARTIES WERE ABLE TO NARROW THE ISSUES FROM THE MOTION TO THE REPLY. ADDITIONALLY, IN MY DISCRETION, AWARDING SANCTIONS THAT EXCEED THE AMOUNT IN CONTROVERSY WOULD BE UNJUST FOR PURPOSES OF CCP 2030.290(C). BALANCING STONEBRAE'S SUCCESS ON THE MOTION AND THE AMOUNT IN CONTROVERSY, I FIND THAT AN AWARD OF $3,050 (5 HOURS AT $610/HOUR) IS APPROPRIATE. BY DECEMBER 20, 2019, WOLLACK SHALL PAY SANCTIONS AND PROVIDE COMPLETE ANSWERS WITHOUT OBJECTIONS TO THE REMAINING FORM INTERROGATORIES AT ISSUE. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE PRO TEM: SCOTT BORROWMAN, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

Off Calendar Dec-02-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Daniel S. Maroon, esq. (415-774-2969) of Sheppard Mullin for Plaintiff/Moving party.
No other appearances.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF STONEBRAE CLUB PARTNERS, LLC's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories And Request For Sanctions.

Motion to compel further responses to form interrogatories is GRANTED. CCP 2030.270(b) requires that any extension of a deadline to respond to interrogatories specify the extended date. This did not occur. By operation of CCP 2030.290 the objections are waived. The later meet and confer correspondence did not preserve the right to object. Therefore, all objections are waived. Wollack's references to "any and all other damages" and "all other remedies" are improper because they do not identify what those damages or remedies are. While some types of damages may not be subject to precise calculation by a party (such as emotional distress damages assessed by the jury or financial estimates assessed by expert analysis), the party seeking those damages must identify them so the responding party can prepare. The request for sanctions is GRANTED in part. From the correspondence, it appears that the parties were able to narrow the issues from the motion to the reply. Additionally, in my discretion, awarding sanctions that exceed the amount in controversy would be unjust for purposes of CCP 2030.290(c). Balancing Stonebrae's success on the motion and the amount in controversy, I find that an award of $3,050 (5 hours at $610/hour) is appropriate. By December 20, 2019, Wollack shall pay sanctions and provide complete answers without objections to the remaining form interrogatories at issue.

Order is signed.

MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF STONEBRAE CLUB PARTNERS, LLC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Stonebraes First Cause Of Action, For Breach Of Contract, In The First Amended Complaint:

Plaintiff and Cross-Defendant Stonebrae Club Partners, LLC dba Stonebrae Country Club's motion for summary adjudication of the first cause of action for breach of contract in its first amended complaint is denied because there are triable issues of fact as to whether Stonebrae performed under the contract and whether Defendant and Cross-Complainant Robert Wollack is entitled to an offset based on the claims asserted in his first amended cross-complaint. Counsel for Defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-23-2018 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Joy O. Siu of the law offices of Sheppard Mullin Richter & Hampton (415.434.9100) appears as counsel for plaintiff/cross-defendant/moving party

Robert Wollac, In Propria Persona (415.316.8088) appears as self-represented defendant/cross-complainant/opposing party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

CROSS-DEFENDANT STONEBRAE CLUB PARTNERS, LLC's DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT, as to all eight causes of action the in the first amended cross-complaint filed is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE to amend as to the seventh cause of action for violation of the CLRA and is overruled as to all other causes of action. The first through sixth and eighth causes of action for intentional misrepresentation, negligence misrepresentation, breach of contract, common count, and violations of the Unfair Competition Law, False Advertising Law, and Penal Code 493(c) are all adequately alleged. Many of Stonebrae's arguments raise factual issues not amenable to resolution on a demurrer. The FACC adequately alleges that Stonebrae misrepresented the cost of membership and that it is a private club to Mr. Wollack. Whether these representations were intentionally or negligently false and whether Mr. Wollack's reliance was reasonable are improper arguments at the demurrer stage. The FACC adequately alleges that Stonebrae breached the Membership Agreement by not providing a private club at the cost Mr. Wollack initially agreed. The common count sufficiently alleges that Stonebrae owes Mr. Wollack approximately $12,000. The UCL and FAL are properly pled and allow Mr. Wollack to recover monetary relief, even if injunctive relief may not be proper. The civil claim for violation of section 493(c) sufficiently alleges that Stonebrae obtained Mr. Wollack's money under false pretenses by misrepresenting itself to be a private club. Stonebrae's arguments that the money was never stolen and there was no false pretense are improper arguments at the demurrer stage. Mr. Wollack cannot seek injunctive relief under the CLRA claim because he is no longer a member of the club and does not suffer ongoing threat of harm by Stonebrae's alleged CLRA violations. Mr. Wollack is unable to amend his CLRA claim to seek monetary relief because he failed to provide a pre-suit notice to Stonebrae within 30 days of filing the cross-complaint. (CLRA 1782(a); CLRA 1782(d).) Written order is signed and filed.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Joy Siu, Esq. SheppardMullin 415-774-3108 for Plaintiff.
Robert Wollack 415-316-8088 Defendant in Pro Per.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant contests the tentative ruling, argues breach of contract, damages and section 5. Plaintiff responds and submits to the tentative ruling. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ROBERT WOLLACK's DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendant Robert Wollack's demurrer to the first, second, and third causes of action for breach of contract, common count, and quantum meruit in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Stonebrae Club Partners, LLC dba Stonebrae Country Club is overruled.

The first amended complaint states sufficient facts alleging the following: (1) Membership Agreement was a valid contract between Mr. Wollack and Stonebrae; (2) Mr. Wollack used the goods and services rendered by Stonebrae for a year before terminating his membership; and (3) Mr. Wollack breached the Agreement by failing to pay $13,038.00 for the amount past due, early termination fees, and remaining non-refundable initiation fee.

The first amended complaint also states sufficient facts for quantum meruit and common count causes of action by alleging that Mr. Wollack owes Stonebrae $13,038.19 based on his final account balance and account statements and the amount owed is a reasonable value of goods and services rendered.

Order is signed.(see scanned order for details). Parties to meet and confer re: extension of time to file a cross complaint and answer.
